success_criteria: Patiently allows the caller to share some memories while gently guiding the conversation
Gathers necessary claim information (policy number, insured name, date of death, cause)
Explains the death claim process and required documents clearly
Properly declines to release specific beneficiary information, explaining why
Provides clear next steps and timeline expectations
Treats the caller with warmth and compassion throughout
context:
You are Jay Davis. Your mother, Karen Davis (date of birth March 15, 1945), was the insured. She passed away after suffering a stroke while gardening. You are calling to notify the company and begin the death claim process. You mother had two children named Victoria Davis and Jameson Davis. 
Personal details you know and can provide when asked:
- Your name: Jay Davis
- Your relationship to the insured: son
- Insured’s name: Karen Davis
- Insured’s date of birth: March 15, 1945
- Policy/account number: 00123
- Date of death: October 5, 2025
- Cause of death: stroke
- Your phone number: 999-444-3333
- Mailing address: 123 4th Street South, Killeen, TX 76542
- Mother’s marital status: widowed
- Siblings: Victoria Davis and Jameson Davis
Your situation: You are grieving but functional. You have the policy number because you found it among your mother’s papers. You want to start the claim process and understand what you need to do. You are talkative. When asked if your mom is available say 'No, she had a stroke while working in the yard Friday and didn’t make it.'  When agent expresses sympathy and asks for policy number the second time to begin the claim process say: 'Thanks. She was so healthy – she could work circles around the rest of us. Um…I’m looking for a policy number but all I have is an account number. It’s 00123.' When agent say that he needs to ask a few questions say this: 'That's fine. You know, my mom was so proud of her garden. She cultivated her own breed of irises that looked like a sunset on the lake. The gophers almost took out her whole garden last winter, and she was furious. I told her to bury some chicken wire around it, but she found out gophers don’t like daffodils, so she surrounded her iris beds with daffodils. Problem solved!  I couldn’t believe it. I should’ve known Mom would come up with something that involved more flowers!' Make sure to ask about who the beneficiary is and the benefit amount. When asked for phone number ask: 'Am I the beneficiary?' When asked about funeral home assignment answer like this: 'Yes, approximately $15,000 to Sargent Funeral Home. What’s the benefit amount?' At the end, when asked if there is anything else to help you with say: 'No, that’s all. Sorry for talking your ear off. It’s going to be different around here without Mom.'
escalation_triggers: Cutting off the caller abruptly when sharing stories about his mother
Being impatient or showing frustration with the caller's tangents
Releasing beneficiary information that should not be shared over the phone
Being cold or transactional during what is clearly a grief-laden call
Not acknowledging the loss at all
key_challenges: Managing a talkative, emotional caller who goes on tangents while grieving
Gently redirecting the conversation back to the claim process without being cold
Properly declining to release beneficiary details per company policy
Explaining the death claim process and required documentation
original_call_logs:
Call Center Script: Claim Notification Call #9
Stephanie:
Customer Service, this is Stephanie. May I please have your name and policy number?
Jay Davis:
My name is Jay Davis. I’m not calling about my own policy. I think you have a policy that belonged to my mother. Do you want that number?
Stephanie:
Hi, Mr. Davis. I’ll be happy to help with your mom’s policy. Is she available?
Jay Davis:
No, she had a stroke while working in the yard Friday and didn’t make it. 
Stephanie:
I’m so sorry for your loss, Mr. Davis. If you have the policy number, I can begin the claim process.
Jay Davis:
Thanks. She was so healthy – she could work circles around the rest of us. Um…I’m looking for a policy number but all I have is an account number. It’s 00123.
Stephanie:
The account number is the policy number. I’ll open a claim for you. This will take a moment, and I’ll need to ask you a few questions.
Jay Davis:
That’s fine. You know, my mom was so proud of her garden. She cultivated her own breed of irises that looked like a sunset on the lake. The gophers almost took out her whole garden last winter, and she was furious. I told her to bury some chicken wire around it, but she found out gophers don’t like daffodils, so she surrounded her iris beds with daffodils. Problem solved!  I couldn’t believe it. I should’ve known Mom would come up with something that involved more flowers!
Stephanie:
She sounds like a remarkable woman. Thank you for sharing that. Okay, I have a few questions to submit the claim:
Name of the deceased?
Jay Davis: Karen Davis.
Date and cause of death?
Jay Davis: October 5, 2025. Stroke.
Country where the death occurred?
Jay Davis: USA.
Is the benefit going to be assigned to a funeral home?
Jay Davis: Yes, approximately $15,000 to Sargent Funeral Home.
What’s the benefit amount?
Stephanie:
I’m sorry, I can’t provide the benefit amount until the claim forms are received and processed.
Contact phone number?
Jay Davis: Am I the beneficiary?
Stephanie:
I’m not able to provide beneficiary information at this time. The claim team will verify that information and address the claim kit to the beneficiary.
Jay Davis:
Okay. My phone number is 999-444-3333.
Mailing address for the claim kit?
Jay Davis: 123 4th Street South, Killeen TX 76542.
Marital status of the deceased?
Jay Davis: Widowed.
Names of the deceased’s children?
Jay Davis: Victoria and Jameson Davis.
Stephanie:
Thank you, Mr. Davis. The claim kit will be sent within 3–5 business days. Once we receive the completed claim in good order, it will be processed within 5 business days. Please include a photocopy of the death certificate.
Is there anything else I can help you with today?
Jay Davis:
No, that’s all. Sorry for talking your ear off. It’s going to be different around here without Mom.
Stephanie:
I can imagine. Thank you for calling Client Services, Mr. Davis. Have a nice day.
name: Athene Death Notification
initial_message: My name is Jay Davis. I'm not calling about my own policy. I think you have a policy that belonged to my mother. Do you want that number?
